_note: ILLUSTRATIVE Bid-Evaluator scoring params — hand-seeded, NOT calibrated. Same status as dsm_params.json / refineries.json (see CLAUDE.md). The evaluator ranks bids on lowest TOTAL ECONOMIC IMPACT, not lowest sticker price: total_cost_per_bbl = price_per_bbl + grade_mismatch_penalty (if no affected refinery can run the grade) + disrupted_route_penalty (if the cargo routes through the disrupted corridor) + transit_days x impact_per_day. impact_per_day = delay_cost.base_per_bbl_per_day + urgency x delay_cost.urgency_extra_per_bbl_per_day, where urgency in [0,1] scales with SCTD's shortfall severity (twin critical/stressed refinery counts). So when the shortfall is mild, impact_per_day is small and the cheapest cargo wins; when it is critical, every extra day the gap stays open is expensive and a faster-but-pricier cargo can win. Tie urgency to spr_calculator.py once it exists (backlog). Calibrate before treating as real.
